name: Kolache with Apricot Filling
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/4 cups of the flour and the yeast.
2. In a saucepan, heat together milk, butter, granulated sugar, lemon peel, and salt until warm (115-120), stirring constantly.
3. Add the mixture to the dry ingredients in the mixing bowl; add eggs.
4. Beat at low speed of an electric mixer for 1/2 minute, scraping the bowl constantly.
5. Beat for 3 minutes at high speed.
6. By hand, stir in enough of the remaining flour to make a moderately soft dough.
7. Knead on lightly floured surface until smooth and elastic (8 to 10 minutes).
8. Shape into a ball and place in a greased bowl; turn once to grease the surface. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled (1 to 1 1/2 hours).
9. Punch down, divide into half. Cover and let rest 10 minutes.
10. Shape each half into 9 balls. Place balls, about 3 inches apart, on greased baking sheets.
11. Flatten each ball to a 3 1/2-inch circle. Cover and let rise until doubled (about 45 minutes).
12. Make a depression in the center of each ball. Fill the depression with Apricot Filling.
13. Bake at 375°F for 10 to 12 minutes. Remove from baking sheets and cool.
14. Dust lightly with powdered sugar.
APRICOT FILLING:
Mix 1 cup dried apricots, snipped, and enough water to come 1 inch above the apricots. Simmer for 15 to 20 minutes; drain. Stir in 1/4 cup granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon butter, and 1/4 teaspoon ground nutmeg.
